name: Lonnie Hillyer
abstract:
Lonnie Hillyer (March 25, 1940 in Monroe, Georgia – July 1, 1985 in New York City) was an American jazz trumpeter, strongly influenced by Dizzy Gillespie, Charlie Parker, Thelonious Monk
and other bebop legends of that era. Lonnie Hillyer moved with his family to Detroit at age three, and began studying music at 14 under Barry Harris.

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Owens
about_quotes.evidence: That was Rolf Ericson, Lonnie Hillyer and myself in the trumpet section.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Owens — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Ray Vega
about_quotes.evidence: one was a harmony teacher named Susan Winder... and then for a short period this guy named Lonnie Hillyer... So these folks really kind of showed me how to like organize my thoughts.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ray Vega interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, July 20, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Charles McPherson
about_quotes.evidence: Lonnie Hillyer, a trumpet player, and myself. We had a gig... that was the rhythm section of a gig many years ago, in the late sixties.
about_quotes.doc_title: Charles McPherson interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, September 9, 2024 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Barry Harris
about_quotes.evidence: Lonnie Hillyer, the other pianist... worked at Arturo’s he’s part of me too... Then came Charles and Lonnie... most of the musicians, they came through me
about_quotes.doc_title: Barry Harris — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Konitz Lee
about_quotes.evidence: Because both Bobby and Lonnie played on the big band at Philharmonic Hall with you. They were in the band too.
about_quotes.doc_title: Konitz Lee — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Howard Johnson
about_quotes.evidence: Mingus was there with a quintet with Charles McPherson, Lonnie Hillyer, Danny Richmond, and Jaki Byard.
about_quotes.doc_title: Howard Johnson interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 14, 2019
